# checks.metadata.MetadataCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.metadata.MetadataCheck }

```python
checks.metadata.MetadataCheck()
```

Check that package metadata is valid and complete.

Validates package metadata according to PEP 621 (pyproject.toml).
Checks for required fields (name, version) and recommends additional
fields (description, readme, license, requires-python).

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("metadata").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

<code>[**REQUIRED_FIELDS**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of fields that must be present in [project].

<code>[**RECOMMENDED_FIELDS**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of fields that should be present.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run the metadata check on a package:

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = MetadataCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.status in [CheckStatus.OK, CheckStatus.NOTE, CheckStatus.ERROR]
True
```

The check validates pyproject.toml structure:

```python
>>> check.REQUIRED_FIELDS
['name', 'version']
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.metadata.MetadataCheck.run) | Check package metadata for validity and completeness. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.metadata.MetadataCheck.run }

```python
checks.metadata.MetadataCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Check package metadata for validity and completeness.

Looks for pyproject.toml first (preferred), falling back to
setup.py/setup.cfg (legacy). Validates required fields and
suggests recommended fields.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory containing pyproject.toml or setup files.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check. Currently no check-specific options are used.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if all required and recommended fields present

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - NOTE status if required fields present but some recommended missing

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if using legacy setup.py/setup.cfg

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - ERROR status if pyproject.toml is invalid or missing required fields
